data.content:
### Setting Up Environment Variables for Local E2E Testing

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/packages/platform/atoms/README.md

This snippet shows the required environment variables to configure the example platform app for local end-to-end testing of Cal.com atoms. It requires OAuth client details and an organization ID from the platform settings dashboard.

```env
NEXT_PUBLIC_X_CAL_ID=""
X_CAL_SECRET_KEY=""
NEXT_PUBLIC_CALCOM_API_URL="https://api.cal.com/v2"
VITE_BOOKER_EMBED_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID=""
VITE_BOOKER_EMBED_API_URL="https://api.cal.com/v2"
ORGANIZATION_ID=""
```

--------------------------------

### Deploy Cal.com on Render

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/README.md

Provides a direct deployment button for Cal.com on Render, simplifying the setup process for users familiar with the platform.

```markdown
[![Deploy to Render](https://render.com/images/deploy-to-render-button.svg)](https://render.com/deploy?repo=https://github.com/calcom/docker)
```

--------------------------------

### Platform Features API

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/api-reference/v2/migration-guide.mdx

Endpoints for platform customers to manage users and webhooks.

```APIDOC
## POST /v2/platform/managed-users

### Description
Create managed users for platform customers.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/v2/platform/managed-users

## GET /v2/platform/managed-users

### Description
List managed users for platform customers.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/v2/platform/managed-users

## POST /v2/platform/managed-users/{id}/refresh

### Description
Refresh user tokens for managed users.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/v2/platform/managed-users/{id}/refresh

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **id** (string) - Required - The unique identifier of the user.

## POST /v2/platform/webhooks

### Description
Create platform-level webhooks.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/v2/platform/webhooks
```

--------------------------------

### Create an Event Type

Source: https://context7.com/calcom/cal.com/llms.txt

Creates a new event type with specified scheduling configurations, locations, and booking fields.

```APIDOC
## POST /v2/event-types

### Description
Creates a new event type with specified scheduling configurations, locations, and booking fields.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
`/v2/event-types`

### Request Body
- **title** (string) - Required - The title of the event type.
- **slug** (string) - Required - A unique slug for the event type URL.
- **description** (string) - Optional - A description of the event type.
- **lengthInMinutes** (integer) - Required - The duration of the event in minutes.
- **locations** (array) - Optional - An array of location objects for the event.
  - **type** (string) - Required - Type of location (e.g., "integration", "phone").
  - **integration** (string) - Optional - The integration name if type is "integration" (e.g., "zoom").
- **bookingFields** (array) - Optional - Custom fields for attendees to fill out during booking.
  - **type** (string) - Required - Type of field (e.g., "text", "textarea").
  - **name** (string) - Required - The internal name of the field.
  - **label** (string) - Required - The label displayed to the user.
  - **required** (boolean) - Optional - Whether the field is mandatory.
- **requiresConfirmation** (boolean) - Optional - Whether bookings require manual confirmation.
- **minimumBookingNotice** (integer) - Optional - Minimum notice period in minutes before a booking.
- **beforeEventBuffer** (integer) - Optional - Buffer time in minutes before the event.
- **afterEventBuffer** (integer) - Optional - Buffer time in minutes after the event.
- **slotInterval** (integer) - Optional - The interval in minutes between available time slots.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "title": "45 Minute Consultation",
  "slug": "45-min-consultation",
  "description": "In-depth consultation session",
  "lengthInMinutes": 45,
  "locations": [
    {
      "type": "integration",
      "integration": "zoom"
    },
    {
      "type": "phone"
    }
  ],
  "bookingFields": [
    {
      "type": "text",
      "name": "company",
      "label": "Company Name",
      "required": true
    },
    {
      "type": "textarea",
      "name": "notes",
      "label": "Additional Notes"
    }
  ],
  "requiresConfirmation": true,
  "minimumBookingNotice": 1440,
  "beforeEventBuffer": 15,
  "afterEventBuffer": 15,
  "slotInterval": 15
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **status** (string) - Indicates the status of the operation (e.g., "success").
- **data** (object) - Contains the details of the created event type.
  - **id** (number) - The internal ID of the event type.
  - **title** (string) - The title of the event type.
  - **slug** (string) - The slug of the event type.
  - **length** (integer) - The duration of the event in minutes.
  - **hidden** (boolean) - Whether the event type is hidden.
  - **position** (integer) - The position of the event type.
  - **userId** (number) - The ID of the user who created the event type.
  - **teamId** (null|number) - The ID of the team, if applicable.
  - **eventName** (null|string) - The name of the event.
  - **timeZone** (null|string) - The time zone for the event.
  - **periodType** (string) - The type of recurring period.
  - **requiresConfirmation** (boolean) - Whether bookings require confirmation.
  - **recurringEvent** (null|object) - Details about recurring events.
  - **disableGuests** (boolean) - Whether to disable guests.
  - **hideCalendarNotes** (boolean) - Whether to hide calendar notes.
  - **minimumBookingNotice** (integer) - Minimum booking notice in minutes.
  - **beforeEventBuffer** (integer) - Buffer time before the event in minutes.
  - **afterEventBuffer** (integer) - Buffer time after the event in minutes.
  - **slotInterval** (integer) - Slot interval in minutes.
  - **locations** (array) - Array of location objects.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "data": {
    "id": 123,
    "title": "45 Minute Consultation",
    "slug": "45-min-consultation",
    "length": 45,
    "hidden": false,
    "position": 0,
    "userId": 1,
    "teamId": null,
    "eventName": null,
    "timeZone": null,
    "periodType": "UNLIMITED",
    "requiresConfirmation": true,
    "recurringEvent": null,
    "disableGuests": true,
    "hideCalendarNotes": false,
    "minimumBookingNotice": 1440,
    "beforeEventBuffer": 15,
    "afterEventBuffer": 15,
    "slotInterval": 15,
    "locations": [
      {"type": "integration", "integration": "zoom"},
      {"type": "phone"}
    ]
  }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Create Booking Reminder Workflow (TypeScript)

Source: https://context7.com/calcom/cal.com/llms.txt

This snippet demonstrates how to create a workflow that sends email and SMS reminders to hosts and attendees before an event. It utilizes Prisma for database interactions and defines custom enums for trigger events and actions. Dependencies include `@prisma/client`.

```typescript
import {
  PrismaClient,
  WorkflowTriggerEvents,
  WorkflowActions
} from '@prisma/client'

const prisma = new PrismaClient()

async function createReminderWorkflow() {
  const workflow = await prisma.workflow.create({
    data: {
      name: 'Booking Reminders',
      userId: 1,
      trigger: WorkflowTriggerEvents.BEFORE_EVENT,
      time: 24, // 24 hours before
      timeUnit: 'HOUR',
      steps: {
        create: [
          {
            stepNumber: 1,
            action: WorkflowActions.EMAIL_HOST,
            template: 'REMINDER',
            emailSubject: 'Upcoming meeting tomorrow',
            reminderBody: 'You have a meeting scheduled for tomorrow at {{start_time}}'
          },
          {
            stepNumber: 2,
            action: WorkflowActions.EMAIL_ATTENDEE,
            template: 'REMINDER',
            emailSubject: 'Reminder: Meeting tomorrow',
            reminderBody: 'Your meeting with {{organizer}} is tomorrow at {{start_time}}'
          },
          {
            stepNumber: 3,
            action: WorkflowActions.SMS_ATTENDEE,
            template: 'REMINDER',
            reminderBody: 'Meeting with {{organizer}} tomorrow at {{start_time}}'
          }
        ]
      },
      activeOn: {
        create: [
          { eventTypeId: 123 },
          { eventTypeId: 456 }
        ]
      }
    },
    include: {
      steps: true,
      activeOn: true
    }
  })

  return workflow
}

// Workflow trigger types
enum WorkflowTriggerEvents {
  BEFORE_EVENT = 'BEFORE_EVENT'
  EVENT_CANCELLED = 'EVENT_CANCELLED'
  NEW_EVENT = 'NEW_EVENT'
  AFTER_EVENT = 'AFTER_EVENT'
  RESCHEDULE_EVENT = 'RESCHEDULE_EVENT'
}

// Available workflow actions
enum WorkflowActions {
  EMAIL_HOST = 'EMAIL_HOST'
  EMAIL_ATTENDEE = 'EMAIL_ATTENDEE'
  SMS_ATTENDEE = 'SMS_ATTENDEE'
  SMS_NUMBER = 'SMS_NUMBER'
  WHATSAPP_ATTENDEE = 'WHATSAPP_ATTENDEE'
  WHATSAPP_NUMBER = 'WHATSAPP_NUMBER'
}
```

--------------------------------

### Connect to GCP VM Instance via SSH

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/self-hosting/deployments/gcp.mdx

This command connects to a Google Cloud Platform (GCP) virtual machine instance using the gcloud CLI. It requires the project ID, zone, and instance name. Ensure you have the gcloud SDK installed and authenticated.

```bash
gcloud ssh --project=[PROJECT_ID] --zone=[ZONE] [INSTANCE_NAME]
```

--------------------------------

### Start Docker Service

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/self-hosting/deployments/gcp.mdx

This command starts the Docker service on a Linux system using systemd. It is necessary for Docker to run background processes and manage containers. You need sudo privileges to execute this command.

```bash
sudo systemctl start docker
```

--------------------------------

### Create and Query Cal.com Bookings with Prisma

Source: https://context7.com/calcom/cal.com/llms.txt

Demonstrates how to create a new booking and query existing bookings with specific filters using PrismaClient in TypeScript. It includes examples for embedding attendee and reference data.

```typescript
// Example booking creation with Prisma
import { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client'

const prisma = new PrismaClient()

async function createBooking() {
  const booking = await prisma.booking.create({
    data: {
      uid: 'bk_' + Math.random().toString(36).substring(7),
      title: '30 Min Meeting',
      description: 'Discussion about project requirements',
      startTime: new Date('2024-01-15T10:00:00Z'),
      endTime: new Date('2024-01-15T10:30:00Z'),
      userId: 1,
      eventTypeId: 123,
      status: 'ACCEPTED',
      attendees: {
        create: [
          {
            email: 'attendee@example.com',
            name: 'John Doe',
            timeZone: 'America/New_York',
            locale: 'en'
          }
        ]
      },
      references: {
        create: [
          {
            type: 'zoom_video',
            uid: 'zoom_meeting_123',
            meetingUrl: 'https://zoom.us/j/123456789'
          }
        ]
      }
    },
    include: {
      attendees: true,
      references: true,
      eventType: true
    }
  })

  return booking
}

// Query bookings with filters
async function getUserBookings(userId: number) {
  const bookings = await prisma.booking.findMany({
    where: {
      userId,
      startTime: {
        gte: new Date()
      },
      status: {
        in: ['ACCEPTED', 'PENDING']
      }
    },
    include: {
      attendees: true,
      eventType: {
        select: {
          title: true,
          length: true
        }
      }
    },
    orderBy: {
      startTime: 'asc'
    }
  })

  return bookings
}
```

--------------------------------

### Create Booking API

Source: https://context7.com/calcom/cal.com/llms.txt

This endpoint allows you to create a new booking for an event type. It supports regular, recurring, and instant bookings.

```APIDOC
## POST /v2/bookings

### Description
Creates a regular, recurring, or instant booking for an event type.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/v2/bookings

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **cal-api-version** (string) - Required - The API version to use (e.g., "2024-08-13").

#### Request Body
- **start** (string) - Required - The start time of the booking in ISO 8601 format.
- **eventTypeId** (integer) - Required - The ID of the event type.
- **instant** (boolean) - Optional - Set to true for instant bookings.
- **attendee** (object) - Required - Information about the attendee.
  - **name** (string) - Required - Attendee's name.
  - **email** (string) - Required - Attendee's email address.
  - **timeZone** (string) - Required - Attendee's time zone (e.g., "America/New_York").
  - **language** (string) - Optional - Attendee's preferred language.
  - **phoneNumber** (string) - Optional - Attendee's phone number.
- **location** (string) - Optional - The location of the meeting (e.g., "integrations:zoom").
- **metadata** (object) - Optional - Additional metadata for the booking.
  - **notes** (string) - Optional - Notes for the booking.
- **bookingFieldsResponses** (object) - Optional - Custom fields for the booking.
  - **company** (string) - Optional - Attendee's company.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "start": "2024-01-15T10:00:00Z",
  "eventTypeId": 123,
  "attendee": {
    "name": "John Doe",
    "email": "john@example.com",
    "timeZone": "America/New_York",
    "language": "en",
    "phoneNumber": "+19876543210"
  },
  "location": "integrations:zoom",
  "metadata": {
    "notes": "Please prepare presentation slides"
  },
  "bookingFieldsResponses": {
    "company": "Acme Corp"
  }
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **status** (string) - The status of the operation (e.g., "success").
- **data** (object) - The created booking details.
  - **id** (integer) - The unique ID of the booking.
  - **uid** (string) - The unique identifier of the booking.
  - **title** (string) - The title of the event.
  - **description** (string) - The description of the event.
  - **hosts** (array) - An array of host objects.
    - **id** (integer) - Host's ID.
    - **name** (string) - Host's name.
    - **email** (string) - Host's email.
    - **timeZone** (string) - Host's time zone.
  - **status** (string) - The status of the booking (e.g., "accepted").
  - **start** (string) - The start time of the booking.
  - **end** (string) - The end time of the booking.
  - **duration** (integer) - The duration of the booking in minutes.
  - **eventTypeId** (integer) - The ID of the event type.
  - **attendees** (array) - An array of attendee objects.
    - **name** (string) - Attendee's name.
    - **email** (string) - Attendee's email.
    - **timeZone** (string) - Attendee's time zone.
    - **absent** (boolean) - Indicates if the attendee is absent.
  - **location** (string) - The location of the meeting.
  - **meetingUrl** (string) - The URL for the meeting.
  - **absentHost** (boolean) - Indicates if the host is absent.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "status": "success",
  "data": {
    "id": 456,
    "uid": "bk_abc123xyz",
    "title": "30 Min Meeting",
    "description": "A 30 minute meeting",
    "hosts": [
      {
        "id": 1,
        "name": "Alice Smith",
        "email": "alice@example.com",
        "timeZone": "America/Los_Angeles"
      }
    ],
    "status": "accepted",
    "start": "2024-01-15T10:00:00.000Z",
    "end": "2024-01-15T10:30:00.000Z",
    "duration": 30,
    "eventTypeId": 123,
    "attendees": [
      {
        "name": "John Doe",
        "email": "john@example.com",
        "timeZone": "America/New_York",
        "absent": false
      }
    ],
    "location": "https://zoom.us/j/123456789",
    "meetingUrl": "https://zoom.us/j/123456789",
    "absentHost": false
  }
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Cal.com EventType Prisma Schema

Source: https://context7.com/calcom/cal.com/llms.txt

Defines the EventType model, including settings for scheduling, booking constraints, hosts, and notifications. It uses Prisma schema definition language.

```prisma
model EventType {
  id                      Int              @id @default(autoincrement())
  title                   String
  slug                    String
  description             String?
  position                Int              @default(0)
  // Booking settings
  locations               Json?
  length                  Int
  offsetStart             Int              @default(0)
  hidden                  Boolean          @default(false)
  // Hosts and assignment
  hosts                   Host[]
  hostGroups              HostGroup[]
  userId                  Int?
  user                    User?            @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  teamId                  Int?
  team                    Team?            @relation(fields: [teamId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  // Scheduling configuration
  schedulingType          SchedulingType?  // ROUND_ROBIN, COLLECTIVE, MANAGED
  hashedLink              HashedLink?
  bookings                Booking[]
  availability            Availability[]
  webhooks                Webhook[]
  destinationCalendar     DestinationCalendar?
  // Confirmation and notifications
  requiresConfirmation    Boolean          @default(false)
  requiresBookerEmailVerification Boolean  @default(false)
  recurringEvent          Json?
  disableGuests           Boolean          @default(false)
  hideCalendarNotes       Boolean          @default(false)
  minimumBookingNotice    Int              @default(120)
  beforeEventBuffer       Int              @default(0)
  afterEventBuffer        Int              @default(0)
  seatsPerTimeSlot        Int?
  onlyShowFirstAvailableSlot Boolean       @default(false)
  seatsShowAttendees      Boolean?         @default(false)
  seatsShowAvailabilityCount Boolean?      @default(true)
  scheduleId              Int?
  schedule                Schedule?        @relation(fields: [scheduleId], references: [id])
  price                   Int              @default(0)
  currency                String           @default("usd")
  slotInterval            Int?
  metadata                Json?
  // Booking limits
  bookingLimits           Json?
  durationLimits          Json?
  // Instant meetings
  isInstantEvent          Boolean          @default(false)
  workflows               WorkflowsOnEventTypes[]
  // Additional features
  assignAllTeamMembers    Boolean          @default(false)
  useEventTypeDestinationCalendarEmail Boolean @default(false)
  isRRWeightsEnabled      Boolean          @default(false)

  @@unique([userId, slug])
  @@unique([teamId, slug])
  @@index([userId])
  @@index([teamId])
}

enum SchedulingType {
  ROUND_ROBIN  // Distributes bookings across hosts
  COLLECTIVE   // All hosts must be available
  MANAGED      // Managed by team admin
}
```
